title: RsaKey caches key_size_bytes at generation time — defensible against lazy per-call get_size
description: RsaKey::generate immediately calls wolfhsm_rsa_get_size and stores the result in key_size_bytes. This value is used by raw_op to pre-size the output buffer to exactly the modulus length, avoiding over-allocation or under-allocation. A contributor might suggest removing the get_size call from generate and computing the buffer size lazily (calling get_size in raw_op, or using a fixed max buffer). The eager approach is correct: it fails fast on an inconsistent server response (key ID returned but size is 0 or negative), and it avoids an extra round-trip on every raw_op call for the same key. The server-confirmed size is also exposed via key_size_bytes() for callers who need it without paying for an extra RPC.
